response.justification: Strong alignment on several fronts: current Staff SWE role at Google involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly matching the AI-native engineering requirement; Sociomantic experience (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency micro-services) demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems depth; and TypeScript runs throughout the career. However, Python is entirely absent from both the skills list and all role descriptions despite being a stated must-have, which is a material gap that needs explaining. A resume entry listing 'Amazon' as the employer for what reads as a personal open-source GIS project also signals carelessness that warrants a closer look.
strengths: Current Google role directly involves AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow tooling — baseline fit with the AI-native engineering environment
Sociomantic role demonstrates genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience at scale (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency micro-services)
Consistent staff-level and technical leadership history across multiple organizations, with evidence of agile/TDD/CI discipline (RIDE Capital, Altom)
concerns: Python is completely absent from skills and all role descriptions — it is listed as a must-have, and the gap needs to be explicitly addressed
Resume contains a factually incorrect entry ('Founder, Amazon, Berlin') for what appears to be a personal open-source project, indicating either carelessness or a data error
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational observability practices — operational readiness is a must-have and the resume is silent on it
